summary: Matched AXDriverUnlink in C by introducing an HSD_SM sound-manager/list-node struct and moving the previous assembly implementation out of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/AXDriver.s. The PR also renamed two previously-address-only AXDriver symbols to HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffAll and HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffTrack and propagated those names through lbaudio_ax.s callsites. The HSD_SM layout was inferred loosely from a K7 version, with the author noting a deliberate difference around the 0x18 field: samID was omitted because the Melee code appears to use that byte as track, supported by an lbz access and surrounding K7-like function ordering.
what_changed: Implemented AXDriverUnlink in src/sysdolphin/baselib/axdriver.c, replacing the assembly body that had lived in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/AXDriver.s.
Added struct HSD_SM to axdriver.h with prev/next links, flags, IDs, track/priority fields, volume/pan/pitch/mix-related fields, curScore, and clock.
Added SMSTATE_MASK, SMSTATE_ACTIVE, and SMSTATE_SLEEP constants in axdriver.h.
Added a forward declaration typedef for HSD_SM in src/sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h.
Added declarations for AXDriverUnlink, AXDriverKeyOff, HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffAll, and HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffTrack to axdriver.h.
Renamed AXDriver_8038BD6C to HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffAll and AXDriver_8038BE64 to HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffTrack in AXDriver.s.
Updated multiple lbaudio_ax.s branch-and-link callsites to use HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffAll and HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffTrack instead of raw AXDriver address-style symbol names.
Added extern AXDriver_80408168 in axdriver.c for the assert message '*head != v'.
smart_moves: Used the linked-list behavior in the existing assembly to identify the first HSD_SM fields as prev and next, then expressed AXDriverUnlink as straightforward C list unlink logic.
Preserved the double check against *head == v and the __assert call, including the source line number 113 and the existing axdriver.c string symbol, rather than simplifying it away.

Renamed raw address symbols only where the diff had strong behavioral evidence: all-track key-off and track-specific key-off routines were reflected by lbaudio_ax.s call contexts and the author's K7 comparison.
Introduced HSD_SM in a header and forward.h at the same time as matching AXDriverUnlink, avoiding local-only typing that would block later audio decompilation work.
Captured uncertainty in the PR body: the HSD_SM layout is based loosely on K7, but the author explicitly documented why samID at 0x18 was not copied into the Melee layout.
decomp_lessons: When matching an unlink function, resist over-abstracting: assign prev and next locals, clear the node links, patch neighboring nodes, update the head pointer, and retain original assert checks in source order.
Struct layouts borrowed from related versions should be treated as evidence, not gospel; confirm fields against Melee loads/stores and nearby function behavior.
A single byte load at a suspected cross-version field can justify renaming or relayout when surrounding call ordering also matches the reference version.
Renaming address-style assembly symbols can improve downstream readability even before the functions themselves are fully decompiled, especially when many callsites share a clear semantic role.
Forward declarations in baselib/forward.h are useful when a new struct type needs to appear in public function prototypes without forcing unnecessary full definitions elsewhere.
naming_conventions: Struct type added as HSD_SM, with typedef declared in forward.h as typedef struct HSD_SM HSD_SM.
State mask constants use uppercase SMSTATE_* names: SMSTATE_MASK, SMSTATE_ACTIVE, SMSTATE_SLEEP.
Audio key-off functions were named with the HSD_AudioSFX prefix: HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffAll and HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffTrack.
AXDriverUnlink and AXDriverKeyOff retain AXDriver-prefixed names because they operate on HSD_SM nodes inside the AX driver subsystem.
Fields use mixed existing-style names visible in the diff, including volumeType, itdflag, curScore, loopcount, and fadetime.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the AXDriverUnlink assembly block after matching it in C.
Matched the assembly's null guard on v before performing list manipulation.
Matched the assembly order: load prev/next, zero v->next and v->prev, update prev->next, update next->prev, update *head if needed, then assert if *head still equals v.
Kept the assert condition as a second independent if (*head == v), mirroring the assembly rather than folding it into an else or unreachable assumption.
Used assembly callsite renames in lbaudio_ax.s to document already-known branch targets without changing code generation.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that refine HSD_SM field names or layout, especially around the omitted samID / track field.
Search for decompiled implementations of HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffAll and HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffTrack.
Look for remaining AXDriver_8038xxxx raw symbols in audio code that may be identifiable through K7 ordering.
Check whether SMSTATE_* constants are reused by later baselib audio matching work.
Search for AXDriverUnlink callsites to confirm HSD_SM list ownership and head pointer semantics.
